Module com.trifasico.x50zo
Package com.trifasico.x50zo.model.players

Interface IPlayer

All Known Implementing Classes:
HumanPlayer, MachinePlayer, PlayerAdapter
public interface IPlayer
Defines the complete behavioral contract for any participant in a Cincuentazo game, whether human or machine.

This interface is part of the event-driven MVC design: the controller calls these methods during turn resolution and relies on the checked exceptions to branch the game flow (e.g. eliminate a player who cannot move, or reject an illegal card choice from the human).

Concrete player types should not implement this interface directly. Instead they should extend PlayerAdapter, which provides default implementations for hand management, leaving each subclass to override only what is specific to it.

Version:
1.0
Author:
Yostin Ramirez, Lesly Zapata, Joseph Terreros
See Also:
  • PlayerAdapter

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

    Checks that the player can move, throwing if they cannot.

    Returns the player's display name.

    Returns the number of cards currently in the player's hand.

    Returns whether the player has at least one card that can legally be played given the current table sum.
    Card

    Plays the card at the given hand index onto the table.
    void
    receiveCard(Card card)
    Adds a card drawn from the deck to this player's hand.

  • Method Details

    • playCard

      Card playCard(int cardIndex, int currentSum) throws InvalidPlayException
      Plays the card at the given hand index onto the table.

      The implementing class must verify that the card is legal before removing it from the hand. If the play is illegal it must throw InvalidPlayException and leave the hand unchanged.

      Parameters:
      cardIndex - zero-based index of the card to play
      currentSum - the running table sum before this play
      Returns:
      the Card that was played and removed from the hand
      Throws:
      InvalidPlayException - (checked) if the selected card would push the sum above 50
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if cardIndex is out of range

    • receiveCard

      void receiveCard(Card card)
      Adds a card drawn from the deck to this player's hand.
      Parameters:
      card - the Card to receive; must not be null

    • getHand

      List<Card> getHand()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the player's current hand.
      Returns:
      the hand as an unmodifiable List; never null

    • hasPlayableCard

      boolean hasPlayableCard(int currentSum)
      Returns whether the player has at least one card that can legally be played given the current table sum.
      Parameters:
      currentSum - the running table sum
      Returns:
      true if at least one card in hand is playable

    • assertCanPlay

      void assertCanPlay(int currentSum) throws NoPlayableCardException
      Checks that the player can move, throwing if they cannot.

      Convenience method used by the turn manager before attempting playCard(int, int). If the player has no legal move the exception triggers the elimination flow.

      Parameters:
      currentSum - the running table sum
      Throws:
      NoPlayableCardException - (checked) if no card in hand is playable

    • getName

      String getName()
      Returns the player's display name.
      Returns:
      the name; never null or empty

    • handSize

      int handSize()
      Returns the number of cards currently in the player's hand.
      Returns:
      hand size (0–4 during normal play)
